records.ety: [AS. sund a swimming, akin to E. swim. See Swim.]

defs: The air bladder of a fish; as, cod sounds are an esteemed article of food.
A cuttlefish.

records.ety: [OE. sound, AS. sund; akin to D. gezond, G. gesund, OHG. gisunt, Dan. & Sw. sund, and perhaps to L. sanus. Cf. Sane.]

defs: Whole; unbroken; unharmed; free from flaw, defect, or decay; perfect of the kind; as, sound timber; sound fruit; a sound tooth; a sound ship.
Healthy; not diseased; not being in a morbid state; -- said of body or mind; as, a sound body; a sound constitution; a sound understanding.
Firm; strong; safe.
quotes: The brasswork here, how rich it is in beams, And how, besides, it makes the whole house sound.

defs: Free from error; correct; right; honest; true; faithful; orthodox; -- said of persons; as, a sound lawyer; a sound thinker.
quotes: Do not I know you a favorer Of this new seat? Ye are nor sound.

defs: Founded in truth or right; supported by justice; not to be overthrown on refuted; not fallacious; as, sound argument or reasoning; a sound objection; sound doctrine; sound principles.
quotes: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me.

defs: heavy; laid on with force; as, a sound beating.
Undisturbed; deep; profound; as, sound sleep.
Founded in law; legal; valid; not defective; as, a sound title to land.
notes: ☞ Sound is sometimes used in the formation of self-explaining compounds; as, sound-headed, sound-hearted, sound-timbered, etc.

defs: Soundly.
quotes: So sound he slept that naught might him awake.

records.ety: [AS. sund a narrow sea or strait; akin to Icel., Sw., Dan. & G. sund, probably so named because it could be swum across. See Swim.]

defs: A narrow passage of water, or a strait between the mainland and an island; also, a strait connecting two seas, or connecting a sea or lake with the ocean; as, the Sound between the Baltic and the german Ocean; Long Island Sound.
quotes: The Sound of Denmark, where ships pay toll.

records.ety: [F. sonder; cf. AS. sundgyrd a sounding rod, sundline a sounding line (see Sound a narrow passage of water).]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sounded; p. pr. & vb. n. Sounding.]

defs: To measure the depth of; to fathom; especially, to ascertain the depth of by means of a line and plummet.
Fig.: To ascertain, or try to ascertain, the thoughts, motives, and purposes of (a person); to examine; to try; to test; to probe.
quotes: I was in jest, And by that offer meant to sound your breast.
I've sounded my Numidians man by man.

defs: To explore, as the bladder or urethra, with a sound; to examine with a sound; also, to examine by auscultation or percussion; as, to sound a patient.
To ascertain the depth of water with a sounding line or other device.
quotes: I sound as a shipman soundeth in the sea with his plummet to know the depth of sea.

defs: Any elongated instrument or probe, usually metallic, by which cavities of the body are sounded or explored, especially the bladder for stone, or the urethra for a stricture.

records.ety: [OE. soun, OF. son, sun, F. son, fr. L. sonus akin to Skr. svana sound, svan to sound, and perh. to E. swan. Cf. Assonant, Consonant, Person, Sonata, Sonnet, Sonorous, Swan.]

defs: The peceived object occasioned by the impulse or vibration of a material substance affecting the ear; a sensation or perception of the mind received through the ear, and produced by the impulse or vibration of the air or other medium with which the ear is in contact; the effect of an impression made on the organs of hearing by an impulse or vibration of the air caused by a collision of bodies, or by other means; noise; report; as, the sound of a drum; the sound of the human voice; a horrid sound; a charming sound; a sharp, high, or shrill sound.
quotes: The warlike sound Of trumpets loud and clarions.

defs: The occasion of sound; the impulse or vibration which would occasion sound to a percipient if present with unimpaired; hence, the theory of vibrations in elastic media such cause sound; as, a treatise on sound.
notes: ☞ In this sense, sounds are spoken of as audible and inaudible.

defs: Noise without signification; empty noise; noise and nothing else.
quotes: Sense and not sound . . . must be the principle.

records.ety: [OE. sounen, sownen, OF. soner, suner, F. sonner, from L. sonare. See Sound a noise.]

defs: To make a noise; to utter a voice; to make an impulse of the air that shall strike the organs of hearing with a perceptible effect.
To be conveyed in sound; to be spread or published; to convey intelligence by sound.
quotes: From you sounded out the word of the Lord.

defs: To make or convey a certain impression, or to have a certain import, when heard; hence, to seem; to appear; as, this reproof sounds harsh; the story sounds like an invention.
quotes: Good sir, why do you start, and seem to fear Things that do sound so fair?
Soun[d]ing in moral virtue was his speech.

defs: To cause to make a noise; to play on; as, to sound a trumpet or a horn; to sound an alarm.
quotes: A bagpipe well could he play and soun[d].

defs: To cause to exit as a sound; as, to sound a note with the voice, or on an instrument.
To order, direct, indicate, or proclain by a sound, or sounds; to give a signal for by a certain sound; as, to sound a retreat; to sound a parley.
quotes: The clock sounded the hour of noon.

defs: To celebrate or honor by sounds; to cause to be reported; to publish or proclaim; as, to sound the praises of fame of a great man or a great exploit.
To examine the condition of (anything) by causing the same to emit sounds and noting their character; as, to sound a piece of timber; to sound a vase; to sound the lungs of a patient.
To signify; to import; to denote.
quotes: Soun[d]ing alway the increase of his winning.
